{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_be5e8d99c0c04ed9aebb7765","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# aide-de-camp\n2\t\n3\t**A personal command interface.** Speak or type a stream-of-consciousness request — spanning multiple projects, questions, and tasks — and receive parallel, organized results on a live browser canvas or in audio.\n4\t\n5\tCLI alias: `adc`\n6\t\n7\t---\n8\t\n9\t## How It Works\n10\t\n11\t1. **Intent routing** — a single fast LLM call segments your utterance into typed intent threads, each tagged with a project, an intent type (`status`, `action`, `brainstorm`, `lookup`, `reminder`, `task`), and an urgency level (`critical`, `high`, `normal`, `low`).\n12\t\n13\t2. **Parallel dispatch** — all threads run concurrently. A compound utterance touching five projects takes as long as the slowest single agent, not their sum.\n14\t\n15\t3. **Fetch strand** — each agent runs deterministic commands against live data sources (kubectl, git, local repos, SSH targets) to gather raw context for its thread.\n16\t\n17\t4. **Synthesize strand** — one LLM call per thread converts the raw data into a 2–3 sentence structured result.\n18\t\n19\t5. **Canvas delivery** — results stream to the browser via SSE and render as HTML cards, one per topic, updated in place as agents resolve.\n20\t\n21\t```\n22\t  Voice / Text Input\n23\t  \"has the pipeline caught up? also what's the ibkr mcp status?\"\n24\t                        │\n25\t                        ▼\n26\t             ┌──────────────────┐\n27\t             │   Intent Router  │  ← one LLM call, splits utterance\n28\t             └──────────────────┘\n29\t                        │\n30\t          ┌─────────────┴──────────────┐\n31\t          ▼                            ▼\n32\t   [agent: pipeline]           [agent: ibkr-mcp]\n33\t   Fetch → Synthesize           Fetch → Synthesize\n34\t          │                            │\n35\t          └──────────────┬─────────────┘\n36\t                         ▼\n37\t              ┌─────────────────────────┐\n38\t              │  Canvas (SSE)           │\n39\t              │  ┌─────────┐ ┌───────┐  │\n40\t              │  │pipeline │ │ibkr   │  │\n41\t              │  │ card    │ │ card  │  │\n42\t              │  └─────────┘ └───────┘  │\n43\t              └─────────────────────────┘\n44\t```\n45\t\n46\t**Task intents** (`task` type) skip fetch/synthesize. Instead, a work item is created and picked up asynchronously by an async worker fleet. When the work completes, the result is pushed to the canvas via the bead watcher daemon.\n47\t\n48\t---\n49\t\n50\t## Features\n51\t\n52\t### Input methods\n53\t- **Text**: browser canvas at `http://localhost:8000/` or CLI: `adc dispatch \"...\"`\n54\t- **Voice**: OpenAI Realtime API (WebRTC) — speak directly into the browser\n55\t\n56\t### Live canvas\n57\tSingle-page dark-themed UI (`src/canvas/index.html`). Results stream in via Server-Sent Events. Each distinct topic (project + intent) gets its own card, updated in place as results arrive. No page reload needed.\n58\t\n59\t### Voice narration\n60\tResults are narrated at urgency-tiered conversational pauses:\n61\t- `critical` — interrupt immediately\n62\t- `high` — next natural pause\n63\t- `normal` — batched delivery\n64\t- `low` — only when idle\n65\t\n66\t### Self-modification and hot-reload\n67\tAll behavior-defining artifacts are plain files, re-read on every invocation:\n68\t- `prompts/router.md`, `prompts/synthesize.md`, `prompts/voice.md`, `prompts/urgency.md`\n69\t- `config/registry.yaml` — project and alias definitions\n70\t- `config/monitoring.yaml` — ambient monitoring rules\n71\t- `data/components.db` — versioned HTML/CSS card templates (SQLite)\n72\t\n73\tA spoken instruction like \"the deploy card is too cluttered\" triggers the `SelfModificationAgent`, which generates a diff, surfaces it for approval, and hot-reloads the artifact. No redeploy needed.\n74\t\n75\t### Project registry\n76\t`config/registry.yaml` defines explicit project entries with cluster targets, namespaces, aliases, and workflow templates. aide-de-camp also auto-discovers git repos at startup. Refresh via `POST /api/v1/environment/refresh`.\n77\t\n78\t### Session persistence\n79\tSession state lives in a SQLite WAL database (`data/session.db`). Tables: `sessions`, `surfaces`, `utterances`, `intents`, `topics`, `results`. No CI build — runs from source.\n222\t\n223\tRelease: bump version in `pyproject.toml` → commit → `git tag vX.Y.Z` → push.\n224\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c56b23e1-196a-40c0-b0c0-5fc5050f6a2c","uuid":"5745f8d5-964e-4dda-8532-6ea649cbe711","timestamp":"2026-07-02T15:13:04.533Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/README.md","content":"# aide-de-camp\n\n**A personal command interface.** Speak or type a stream-of-consciousness request — spanning multiple projects, questions, and tasks — and receive parallel, organized results on a live browser canvas or in audio.\n\nCLI alias: `adc`\n\n---\n\n## How It Works\n\n1. **Intent routing** — a single fast LLM call segments your utterance into typed intent threads, each tagged with a project, an intent type (`status`, `action`, `brainstorm`, `lookup`, `reminder`, `task`), and an urgency level (`critical`, `high`, `normal`, `low`).\n\n2. **Parallel dispatch** — all threads run concurrently. A compound utterance touching five projects takes as long as the slowest single agent, not their sum.\n\n3. **Fetch strand** — each agent runs deterministic commands against live data sources (kubectl, git, local repos, SSH targets) to gather raw context for its thread.\n\n4. **Synthesize strand** — one LLM call per thread converts the raw data into a 2–3 sentence structured result.\n\n5. **Canvas delivery** — results stream to the browser via SSE and render as HTML cards, one per topic, updated in place as agents resolve.\n\n```\n  Voice / Text Input\n  \"has the pipeline caught up? also what's the ibkr mcp status?\"\n                        │\n                        ▼\n             ┌──────────────────┐\n             │   Intent Router  │  ← one LLM call, splits utterance\n             └──────────────────┘\n                        │\n          ┌─────────────┴──────────────┐\n          ▼                            ▼\n   [agent: pipeline]           [agent: ibkr-mcp]\n   Fetch → Synthesize           Fetch → Synthesize\n          │                            │\n          └──────────────┬─────────────┘\n                         ▼\n              ┌─────────────────────────┐\n              │  Canvas (SSE)           │\n              │  ┌─────────┐ ┌───────┐  │\n              │  │pipeline │ │ibkr   │  │\n              │  │ card    │ │ card  │  │\n              │  └─────────┘ └───────┘  │\n              └─────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n**Task intents** (`task` type) skip fetch/synthesize. Instead, a work item is created and picked up asynchronously by an async worker fleet. When the work completes, the result is pushed to the canvas via the bead watcher daemon.\n\n---\n\n## Features\n\n### Input methods\n- **Text**: browser canvas at `http://localhost:8000/` or CLI: `adc dispatch \"...\"`\n- **Voice**: OpenAI Realtime API (WebRTC) — speak directly into the browser\n\n### Live canvas\nSingle-page dark-themed UI (`src/canvas/index.html`). Results stream in via Server-Sent Events. Each distinct topic (project + intent) gets its own card, updated in place as results arrive. No page reload needed.\n\n### Voice narration\nResults are narrated at urgency-tiered conversational pauses:\n- `critical` — interrupt immediately\n- `high` — next natural pause\n- `normal` — batched delivery\n- `low` — only when idle\n\n### Self-modification and hot-reload\nAll behavior-defining artifacts are plain files, re-read on every invocation:\n- `prompts/router.md`, `prompts/synthesize.md`, `prompts/voice.md`, `prompts/urgency.md`\n- `config/registry.yaml` — project and alias definitions\n- `config/monitoring.yaml` — ambient monitoring rules\n- `data/components.db` — versioned HTML/CSS card templates (SQLite)\n\nA spoken instruction like \"the deploy card is too cluttered\" triggers the `SelfModificationAgent`, which generates a diff, surfaces it for approval, and hot-reloads the artifact. No redeploy needed.\n\n### Project registry\n`config/registry.yaml` defines explicit project entries with cluster targets, namespaces, aliases, and workflow templates. aide-de-camp also auto-discovers git repos at startup. Refresh via `POST /api/v1/environment/refresh`.\n\n### Session persistence\nSession state lives in a SQLite WAL database (`data/session.db`). Tables: `sessions`, `surfaces`, `utterances`, `intents`, `topics`, `results`. The same session is accessible from the canvas, CLI, or audio mode simultaneously.\n\n---\n\n## Quick Start\n\n```bash\n# Clone and install\ngit clone <repo-url> aide-de-camp\ncd aide-de-camp\n\npip install -e \".[dev]\"\n# or\npip install -r requirements.txt\n\n# Start the server\nuvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000\n\n# Verify it's running\ncurl -s http://localhost:8000/health\n```\n\nOpen `http://localhost:8000/` in your browser to use the canvas.\n\nFor voice mode, an OpenAI API key with Realtime API access is required (set `OPENAI_API_KEY`).\n\n---\n\n## CLI Usage\n\n```bash\n# Route an utterance and stream results to the canvas\nadc dispatch \"has the options pipeline caught up?\"\n\n# Query a specific topic\nadc ask \"what name candidates did we have for pdftract?\"\n\n# Current session summary\nadc status\n\n# List active topic cards\nadc topics\n\n# Show exception queue\nadc exceptions\n\n# Configure the CLI\nadc config --set-server http://myhost:8000\nadc config --set-session <session-id>\n\n# Help\nadc --help\n```\n\n---\n\n## Configuration\n\n### Environment variables\n\n| Variable | Purpose | Default |\n|----------|---------|---------|\n| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI key — required for voice/Realtime API | _(none; voice disabled without it)_ |\n| `ADC_SERVER_URL` | Server URL used by the `adc` CLI | `http://localhost:8000` |\n| `ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL` | Telegram bridge URL for async task notifications | `https://telegram-proxy-telegram-bridge-ardenone-cluster-ts.ardenone.com:8444` |\n\nThe LLM backend for intent routing and synthesis is configurable via `config/registry.yaml`. By default it points to a local proxy endpoint — update this to any OpenAI-compatible API endpoint.\n\n### Prompts directory\n\nAll prompts are hot-reloaded on every invocation. Edit these files to tune behavior without restarting the server:\n\n| File | Purpose |\n|------|---------|\n| `prompts/router.md` | Intent classification and thread segmentation |\n| `prompts/synthesize.md` | Result synthesis (raw data → 2–3 sentence summary) |\n| `prompts/voice.md` | Narration style and pacing |\n| `prompts/urgency.md` | Urgency classification rules |\n\n### Project registry\n\n`config/registry.yaml` defines the projects aide-de-camp knows about. Each entry specifies:\n- `name` and `aliases` — how the router maps utterance fragments to projects\n- `cluster` and `namespace` — where to run kubectl-based fetch commands\n- `workflow_templates` — named multi-step workflows (e.g., \"deploy\", \"rollback\")\n\n---\n\n## Architecture\n\n### Key source files\n\n| File | Responsibility |\n|------|---------------|\n| `src/main.py` | FastAPI app; `/dispatch`, `/voice`, `/sse` endpoints |\n| `src/intent/router.py` | LLM classification and parallel thread dispatch |\n| `src/fetch/commands.py` | Fetch command matrix per intent type |\n| `src/fetch/orchestrator.py` | Parallel fetch execution |\n| `src/synthesize/strand.py` | LLM synthesis strand |\n| `src/session/store.py` | SQLite session store (aiosqlite) |\n| `src/sse/broadcaster.py` | SSE connection registry and event routing |\n| `src/canvas/index.html` | Canvas single-page app |\n| `src/realtime/session.py` | OpenAI Realtime API voice session |\n| `src/watcher/daemon.py` | Async work-item watcher — pushes completed results to canvas |\n| `src/agents/self_modification.py` | Self-improvement agent |\n| `src/environment/discovery.py` | Local and remote repo scanner |\n| `config/registry.yaml` | Explicit project registry |\n| `prompts/` | Hot-reload prompt files |\n\n### Runtime breakdown\n\n| Runtime | Used for |\n|---------|---------|\n| Realtime API | Voice model (persistent session, voice I/O) |\n| Configurable LLM backend | Intent router, synthesize strand |\n| Deterministic code | Fetch strand (kubectl/git execution), bead watcher |\n| Async worker fleet | UI-regen agent, self-modification agent, background analysis, task work items |\n\n### Data flow\n\n```\nPOST /dispatch\n  → intent router (LLM classify)\n  → N parallel: fetch strand → synthesize strand\n  → persist: topic + result in session.db\n  → broadcast SSEEvent(\"result_created\", surface_id)\n  → canvas calls GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics\n  → cards rendered / updated in place\n```\n\n---\n\n## Requirements\n\n- Python 3.11+\n- `fastapi`, `uvicorn[standard]`, `httpx`, `websockets`, `pydantic`, `aiosqlite`\n- Dev: `pytest`, `pytest-asyncio`, `ruff`\n\n---\n\n## Versioning\n\nVersion is in `pyproject.toml` only. No CI build — runs from source.\n\nRelease: bump version in `pyproject.toml` → commit → `git tag vX.Y.Z` → push.\n","numLines":224,"startLine":1,"totalLines":224}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_42af0e5517b34d38b646254b","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# telegram-claude-bridge\n2\t\n3\tA bridge that connects a Telegram bot to [Claude Code](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code) CLI sessions, letting users interact with Claude directly from Telegram group forum topics. Each topic gets a persistent Claude Code session running in tmux; the bridge routes messages in, streams responses back, and keeps sessions warm between turns. Uses Anthropic subscription billing (interactive PTY), not API credits.\n4\t\n5\t---\n6\t\n7\t## How it works\n8\t\n9\tThe system is split into two processes:\n10\t\n11\t**Proxy** — A lightweight container that holds the Telegram bot token, long-polls the Telegram `getUpdates` endpoint, and exposes an internal HTTP API (`/updates`, `/send`, `/edit`, etc.) for the bridge to consume. It holds no session state.\n12\t\n13\t**Bridge** — The stateful brain, running as a systemd service on the bare-metal host. It polls the proxy, manages Claude Code sessions, spawns `claude` inside tmux panes (one pane per forum topic), streams responses back via the proxy, and stores session metadata in a local SQLite database.\n14\t\n15\t**Dashboard** (optional) — A Bubbletea TUI that displays real-time session health, in-flight messages, and cost data over a Unix socket.\n16\t\n17\t### Claude invocation\n18\t\n19\tThe bridge maintains a tmux session named `telegram-bridge`. Each active Telegram forum topic maps to one tmux window. Claude Code is launched with configurable permissions:\n20\t\n21\t```\n22\tclaude <permission_flags> --model <model> [--resume <session_id>]\n23\t```\n24\t\n25\tPermission flags are determined by the group's `permission_mode` setting:\n26\t- `bypassPermissions` → `--dangerously-skip-permissions`\n27\t- `acceptEdits`, `plan`, `dontAsk` → `--permission-mode <mode>`\n28\t\n29\t**Note:** The current implementation always passes `--dangerously-skip-permissions` regardless of the stored `permission_mode` (tracked issue). The `permission_mode` is configurable via `/config permission_mode <mode>` or `/permission <mode>`, but does not yet affect runtime behavior.\n30\t\n31\tPanes stay warm between messages (45 s idle threshold). A Claude stop-hook writes the final response to a file that the bridge polls; it falls back to PTY screen-scraping if the hook is not configured. On session ID loss, up to 40 messages of history are prepended from SQLite to restore context.\n32\t\n33\t---\n34\t\n35\t## Features\n36\t\n37\t### Notification modes\n38\t\n39\tPer-topic, configurable via `/notify` or natural language:\n40\t\n41\t| Mode | Behavior |\n42\t|------|----------|\n43\t| `live` (default) | Progressively edits one Telegram message as Claude streams (1 s debounce) |\n44\t| `summary` | Posts a placeholder, edits to the final response only when done |\n45\t| `quiet` | No updates during processing; posts only on completion |\n46\t\n47\t### Natural language intent detection\n48\t\n49\tThe bridge intercepts common phrases before forwarding them to Claude:\n50\t\n51\t- **Cancel:** \"cancel\", \"stop\", \"abort\"\n52\t- **Model switch:** \"use opus\", \"think harder\", \"fast mode\", etc.\n53\t- **Notify mode:** \"stream updates\", \"quiet mode\", etc.\n54\t- **Cost / status queries**\n55\t- **Session control:** \"close session\", \"new session\"\n56\t- **Timeout adjustments**\n57\t\n58\t### Media support\n59\t\n60\t| Type | Handling |\n61\t|------|----------|\n62\t| Photos | Injected as image attachments (10 MB limit) |\n63\t| Voice messages | Transcribed via `whisper` CLI, text prepended to prompt |\n64\t| Audio files (MP3, M4A, FLAC, WAV, OGG) | Same as voice messages |\n65\t| Video / video notes | Keyframes extracted + audio transcribed; both fed to Claude |\n66\t| Documents | Passed as file attachments (50 MB limit) |\n67\t\n68\t### Dispatcher / orchestrator mode\n69\t\n70\tWhen enabled (`/dispatch on`), Claude receives two synthetic tools:\n71\t\n72\t- `spawn_worker` — the bridge spawns a headless Claude instance and injects the result back\n73\t- `update_progress` — posts a status message to the topic immediately\n74\t\n75\t### Self-update\n76\t\n77\tThe bridge checks a configurable remote for new versions on a background interval and applies updates with `/update do`. Update checking can be disabled by setting `UPDATE_INTERVAL_MINUTES=0`.\n78\t\n79\t### Session lifecycle\n80\t\n81\t- One session per `(chat_id, thread_id)` pair\n82\t- Messages arriving during processing are batched in a per-topic queue (32 messages deep)\n83\t- Sessions resume across restarts via `--resume <session_id>`\n84\t- Stale sessions cleaned up automatically (configurable interval and TTL)\n85\t- On close: Claude (Haiku) generates a summary and pins it to the topic\n86\t\n87\t---\n88\t\n89\t## Prerequisites\n90\t\n91\tThe following must be available on the host running the bridge:\n92\t\n93\t- **tmux** — session multiplexer that hosts Claude Code panes\n94\t- **claude** — [Claude Code CLI](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code), logged in with an active Anthropic subscription\n95\t- **whisper** — [whisper.cpp](https://github.com/ggerganov/whisper.cpp) or compatible CLI (required only for voice/audio transcription)\n96\t- **git** — required only if self-update is enabled\n97\t\n98\tThe proxy runs as a Docker container and has no host-level dependencies beyond a container runtime.\n99\t\n100\t---\n101\t\n102\t## Configuration\n103\t\n104\t### Proxy environment variables\n105\t\n106\t| Variable | Default | Description |\n107\t|----------|---------|-------------|\n108\t| `BOT_TOKEN` (or `TELEGRAM_TOKEN`) | — | **Required.** Telegram bot token. |\n109\t| `PROXY_LISTEN_ADDR` | `:8080` | HTTP listen address |\n110\t| `PROXY_DB_PATH` | `proxy.db` | SQLite DB path for offset tracking |\n111\t| `POLL_TIMEOUT` | `30` | Telegram long-poll timeout (seconds) |\n112\t\n113\t### Bridge environment variables\n114\t\n115\t| Variable | Default | Description |\n116\t|----------|---------|-------------|\n117\t| `PROXY_URL` | — | **Required.** Base URL of the proxy (e.g. `http://localhost:8080`) |\n118\t| `BRIDGE_DB_PATH` | `bridge.db` | SQLite DB path |\n119\t| `ALLOWED_CHAT_ID` | `0` (all) | Restrict to a single Telegram chat ID |\n120\t| `POLL_TIMEOUT` | `30` | Long-poll timeout (seconds) |\n121\t| `SESSION_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MINUTES` | `60` | Stale session cleanup interval (`0` = disabled) |\n122\t| `SESSION_TTL_HOURS` | `168` (7 days) Send `/cwd <path>` from the admin account to register the group and set the working directory.\n260\t\n261\t---\n262\t\n263\t## Version\n264\t\n265\tCurrent version: **0.3.0**\n266\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c56b23e1-196a-40c0-b0c0-5fc5050f6a2c","uuid":"01dd3be8-23a0-4b1a-aebb-33e0ae2d1702","timestamp":"2026-07-02T15:14:27.806Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/telegram-claude-bridge/README.md","content":"# telegram-claude-bridge\n\nA bridge that connects a Telegram bot to [Claude Code](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code) CLI sessions, letting users interact with Claude directly from Telegram group forum topics. Each topic gets a persistent Claude Code session running in tmux; the bridge routes messages in, streams responses back, and keeps sessions warm between turns. Uses Anthropic subscription billing (interactive PTY), not API credits.\n\n---\n\n## How it works\n\nThe system is split into two processes:\n\n**Proxy** — A lightweight container that holds the Telegram bot token, long-polls the Telegram `getUpdates` endpoint, and exposes an internal HTTP API (`/updates`, `/send`, `/edit`, etc.) for the bridge to consume. It holds no session state.\n\n**Bridge** — The stateful brain, running as a systemd service on the bare-metal host. It polls the proxy, manages Claude Code sessions, spawns `claude` inside tmux panes (one pane per forum topic), streams responses back via the proxy, and stores session metadata in a local SQLite database.\n\n**Dashboard** (optional) — A Bubbletea TUI that displays real-time session health, in-flight messages, and cost data over a Unix socket.\n\n### Claude invocation\n\nThe bridge maintains a tmux session named `telegram-bridge`. Each active Telegram forum topic maps to one tmux window. Claude Code is launched with configurable permissions:\n\n```\nclaude <permission_flags> --model <model> [--resume <session_id>]\n```\n\nPermission flags are determined by the group's `permission_mode` setting:\n- `bypassPermissions` → `--dangerously-skip-permissions`\n- `acceptEdits`, `plan`, `dontAsk` → `--permission-mode <mode>`\n\n**Note:** The current implementation always passes `--dangerously-skip-permissions` regardless of the stored `permission_mode` (tracked issue). The `permission_mode` is configurable via `/config permission_mode <mode>` or `/permission <mode>`, but does not yet affect runtime behavior.\n\nPanes stay warm between messages (45 s idle threshold). A Claude stop-hook writes the final response to a file that the bridge polls; it falls back to PTY screen-scraping if the hook is not configured. On session ID loss, up to 40 messages of history are prepended from SQLite to restore context.\n\n---\n\n## Features\n\n### Notification modes\n\nPer-topic, configurable via `/notify` or natural language:\n\n| Mode | Behavior |\n|------|----------|\n| `live` (default) | Progressively edits one Telegram message as Claude streams (1 s debounce) |\n| `summary` | Posts a placeholder, edits to the final response only when done |\n| `quiet` | No updates during processing; posts only on completion |\n\n### Natural language intent detection\n\nThe bridge intercepts common phrases before forwarding them to Claude:\n\n- **Cancel:** \"cancel\", \"stop\", \"abort\"\n- **Model switch:** \"use opus\", \"think harder\", \"fast mode\", etc.\n- **Notify mode:** \"stream updates\", \"quiet mode\", etc.\n- **Cost / status queries**\n- **Session control:** \"close session\", \"new session\"\n- **Timeout adjustments**\n\n### Media support\n\n| Type | Handling |\n|------|----------|\n| Photos | Injected as image attachments (10 MB limit) |\n| Voice messages | Transcribed via `whisper` CLI, text prepended to prompt |\n| Audio files (MP3, M4A, FLAC, WAV, OGG) | Same as voice messages |\n| Video / video notes | Keyframes extracted + audio transcribed; both fed to Claude |\n| Documents | Passed as file attachments (50 MB limit) |\n\n### Dispatcher / orchestrator mode\n\nWhen enabled (`/dispatch on`), Claude receives two synthetic tools:\n\n- `spawn_worker` — the bridge spawns a headless Claude instance and injects the result back\n- `update_progress` — posts a status message to the topic immediately\n\n### Self-update\n\nThe bridge checks a configurable remote for new versions on a background interval and applies updates with `/update do`. Update checking can be disabled by setting `UPDATE_INTERVAL_MINUTES=0`.\n\n### Session lifecycle\n\n- One session per `(chat_id, thread_id)` pair\n- Messages arriving during processing are batched in a per-topic queue (32 messages deep)\n- Sessions resume across restarts via `--resume <session_id>`\n- Stale sessions cleaned up automatically (configurable interval and TTL)\n- On close: Claude (Haiku) generates a summary and pins it to the topic\n\n---\n\n## Prerequisites\n\nThe following must be available on the host running the bridge:\n\n- **tmux** — session multiplexer that hosts Claude Code panes\n- **claude** — [Claude Code CLI](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code), logged in with an active Anthropic subscription\n- **whisper** — [whisper.cpp](https://github.com/ggerganov/whisper.cpp) or compatible CLI (required only for voice/audio transcription)\n- **git** — required only if self-update is enabled\n\nThe proxy runs as a Docker container and has no host-level dependencies beyond a container runtime.\n\n---\n\n## Configuration\n\n### Proxy environment variables\n\n| Variable | Default | Description |\n|----------|---------|-------------|\n| `BOT_TOKEN` (or `TELEGRAM_TOKEN`) | — | **Required.** Telegram bot token. |\n| `PROXY_LISTEN_ADDR` | `:8080` | HTTP listen address |\n| `PROXY_DB_PATH` | `proxy.db` | SQLite DB path for offset tracking |\n| `POLL_TIMEOUT` | `30` | Telegram long-poll timeout (seconds) |\n\n### Bridge environment variables\n\n| Variable | Default | Description |\n|----------|---------|-------------|\n| `PROXY_URL` | — | **Required.** Base URL of the proxy (e.g. `http://localhost:8080`) |\n| `BRIDGE_DB_PATH` | `bridge.db` | SQLite DB path |\n| `ALLOWED_CHAT_ID` | `0` (all) | Restrict to a single Telegram chat ID |\n| `POLL_TIMEOUT` | `30` | Long-poll timeout (seconds) |\n| `SESSION_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MINUTES` | `60` | Stale session cleanup interval (`0` = disabled) |\n| `SESSION_TTL_HOURS` | `168` (7 days) | Age at which a session is considered stale |\n| `CLOSE_INACTIVE_TOPICS` | `false` | Close Telegram topics for stale sessions |\n| `ADMIN_USER_ID` | `0` | Bootstrap initial admin on startup |\n| `ADMIN_CHAT_ID` | `0` | Chat ID for crash-loop alerts (via ExecStopPost) |\n| `EVENT_PUBLISHING_ENABLED` | `false` | Enable dashboard Unix socket events |\n| `EVENT_SOCKET_PATH` | `/tmp/telegram-bridge-events.sock` | Dashboard socket path |\n| `UPDATE_INTERVAL_MINUTES` | `5` | Self-update check interval (`0` = disabled) |\n\n---\n\n## Commands reference\n\n### User commands\n\n| Command | Description |\n|---------|-------------|\n| `/new <name>` | Create a new forum topic and start a Claude Code session |\n| `/cwd` | Show the current working directory for this session |\n| `/model [name]` | View or set the active model |\n| `/haiku` | Switch to Claude Haiku |\n| `/sonnet` | Switch to Claude Sonnet |\n| `/opus` | Switch to Claude Opus |\n| `/color [name]` | Set topic icon color (`active`, `complete`, `blocked`, `error`, `review`, `research`) |\n| `/notify [mode]` | Set notification mode (`live`, `summary`, `quiet`) |\n| `/context <thread_id>` | Inject context from another topic into this session |\n| `/snippet <name> <content>` | Save a named context snippet |\n| `/snippets` | List saved snippets |\n| `/info` | Show session details (model, cwd, session ID, message count, cost, notify mode, timeout) |\n| `/status` | List active sessions in this group |\n| `/sessions` | List all sessions across all groups |\n| `/close <thread_id>` | Close a session (generates and pins a summary) |\n| `/cancel [thread_id]` | Cancel the running request |\n| `/dispatch [on\\|off\\|default]` | Toggle orchestrator mode |\n| `/timeout [N]` | Set per-topic timeout in seconds (`0` = use group default) |\n| `/cost` | Show cost breakdown (group total / daily trend / per-topic / per-user) |\n| `/budget [amount]` | View or set the group budget |\n| `/parallel <prompts>` | Run up to 5 prompts in parallel (separate with `---` on its own line) |\n| `/bg <command>` | Run a shell command in the background and stream output to the topic |\n| `/jobs` | List background jobs |\n| `/kill <job_id>` | Kill a background job |\n| `/ping` | Check proxy latency |\n| `/version` | Show bridge and proxy versions |\n| `/help` | Show help |\n\n### Admin-only commands\n\n| Command | Description |\n|---------|-------------|\n| `/cwd <path>` | Set group working directory (also registers the group) |\n| `/permission [mode]` | Set Claude permission mode |\n| `/config [setting] [value]` | View or set `permission_mode`, `allowed_tools`, or `disallowed_tools` |\n| `/update [do]` | Check for or apply a self-update |\n| `/adduser <id> [role]` | Add an allowed user |\n| `/removeuser <id>` | Remove a user |\n| `/users` | List allowed users |\n\nRate limiting: 30 messages per minute per user.\n\n---\n\n## Building\n\nRequires Go 1.25+.\n\n```bash\n# Build all three binaries (bin/proxy, bin/bridge, bin/dashboard)\nmake build\n\n# Build individual components\nmake proxy\nmake bridge\nmake dashboard\n\n# Run tests\nmake test\n\n# Vet\nmake vet\n\n# Build the proxy Docker image\nmake docker\n```\n\nThe resulting Docker image is scratch-based and approximately 5 MB.\n\n---\n\n## Deployment\n\n### Proxy (Docker container)\n\nThe proxy runs as a stateless container. It only needs the bot token and a writable path for its offset-tracking database.\n\n```yaml\n# Example docker-compose snippet\nservices:\n  proxy:\n    image: telegram-claude-bridge:<version>\n    environment:\n      BOT_TOKEN: \"<your-telegram-bot-token>\"\n      PROXY_LISTEN_ADDR: \":8080\"\n      PROXY_DB_PATH: \"/data/proxy.db\"\n    volumes:\n      - ./proxy-data:/data\n    ports:\n      - \"8080:8080\"\n```\n\n### Bridge (systemd service)\n\nThe bridge runs directly on the host so it has access to `tmux`, `claude`, and the user's Anthropic session. A sample unit file is in `deploy/telegram-claude-bridge.service`.\n\n```ini\n[Unit]\nDescription=Telegram Claude Bridge\nAfter=network.target\n\n[Service]\nType=notify\nWatchdogSec=60\nEnvironmentFile=/etc/telegram-claude-bridge/env\nExecStart=/usr/local/bin/bridge\nRestart=on-failure\n\n[Install]\nWantedBy=multi-user.target\n```\n\nThe bridge implements `sd_notify` (`Type=notify`) with a 60 s watchdog, so systemd will restart it automatically if it hangs.\n\n### Telegram bot setup\n\n1. Create a bot via [@BotFather](https://t.me/BotFather) and copy the token.\n2. Enable **Groups** and **Group Admin** permissions for the bot.\n3. In your Telegram group, enable **Topics** (Supergroup setting).\n4. Add the bot to the group and promote it so it can manage topics and pin messages.\n5. Set `ADMIN_USER_ID` to your Telegram user ID to bootstrap the initial admin on first start.\n6. Send `/cwd <path>` from the admin account to register the group and set the working directory.\n\n---\n\n## Version\n\nCurrent version: **0.3.0**\n","numLines":266,"startLine":1,"totalLines":266}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_bfcb6f2e67b04dcb855c5666","type":"tool_result","content":"The file /home/coding/aide-de-camp/README.md has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c56b23e1-196a-40c0-b0c0-5fc5050f6a2c","uuid":"92cc1ae9-f5d9-484b-9797-6b2b894a224c","timestamp":"2026-07-02T15:17:54.038Z","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/README.md","oldString":"| Variable | Purpose | Default |\n|----------|---------|---------|\n| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI key — required for voice/Realtime API | _(none; voice disabled without it)_ |\n| `ADC_SERVER_URL` | Server URL used by the `adc` CLI | `http://localhost:8000` |\n| `ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL` | Telegram bridge URL for async task notifications | `https://telegram-proxy-telegram-bridge-ardenone-cluster-ts.ardenone.com:8444` |","newString":"| Variable | Purpose | Default |\n|----------|---------|---------|\n| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI key — required for voice/Realtime API | _(none; voice disabled without it)_ |\n| `ADC_SERVER_URL` | Server URL used by the `adc` CLI | `http://localhost:8000` |\n| `ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL` | Telegram bridge URL for async task notifications | `https://telegram-proxy-telegram-bridge-ardenone-cluster-ts.ardenone.com:8444` ✓ |","originalFile":"# aide-de-camp\n\n**A personal command interface.** Speak or type a stream-of-consciousness request — spanning multiple projects, questions, and tasks — and receive parallel, organized results on a live browser canvas or in audio.\n\nCLI alias: `adc`\n\n---\n\n## How It Works\n\n1. **Intent routing** — a single fast LLM call segments your utterance into typed intent threads, each tagged with a project, an intent type (`status`, `action`, `brainstorm`, `lookup`, `reminder`, `task`), and an urgency level (`critical`, `high`, `normal`, `low`).\n\n2. **Parallel dispatch** — all threads run concurrently. A compound utterance touching five projects takes as long as the slowest single agent, not their sum.\n\n3. **Fetch strand** — each agent runs deterministic commands against live data sources (kubectl, git, local repos, SSH targets) to gather raw context for its thread.\n\n4. **Synthesize strand** — one LLM call per thread converts the raw data into a 2–3 sentence structured result.\n\n5. **Canvas delivery** — results stream to the browser via SSE and render as HTML cards, one per topic, updated in place as agents resolve.\n\n```\n  Voice / Text Input\n  \"has the pipeline caught up? also what's the ibkr mcp status?\"\n                        │\n                        ▼\n             ┌──────────────────┐\n             │   Intent Router  │  ← one LLM call, splits utterance\n             └──────────────────┘\n                        │\n          ┌─────────────┴──────────────┐\n          ▼                            ▼\n   [agent: pipeline]           [agent: ibkr-mcp]\n   Fetch → Synthesize           Fetch → Synthesize\n          │                            │\n          └──────────────┬─────────────┘\n                         ▼\n              ┌─────────────────────────┐\n              │  Canvas (SSE)           │\n              │  ┌─────────┐ ┌───────┐  │\n              │  │pipeline │ │ibkr   │  │\n              │  │ card    │ │ card  │  │\n              │  └─────────┘ └───────┘  │\n              └─────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n**Task intents** (`task` type) skip fetch/synthesize. Instead, a work item is created and picked up asynchronously by an async worker fleet. When the work completes, the result is pushed to the canvas via the bead watcher daemon.\n\n---\n\n## Features\n\n### Input methods\n- **Text**: browser canvas at `http://localhost:8000/` or CLI: `adc dispatch \"...\"`\n- **Voice**: OpenAI Realtime API (WebRTC) — speak directly into the browser\n\n### Live canvas\nSingle-page dark-themed UI (`src/canvas/index.html`). Results stream in via Server-Sent Events. Each distinct topic (project + intent) gets its own card, updated in place as results arrive. No page reload needed.\n\n### Voice narration\nResults are narrated at urgency-tiered conversational pauses:\n- `critical` — interrupt immediately\n- `high` — next natural pause\n- `normal` — batched delivery\n- `low` — only when idle\n\n### Self-modification and hot-reload\nAll behavior-defining artifacts are plain files, re-read on every invocation:\n- `prompts/router.md`, `prompts/synthesize.md`, `prompts/voice.md`, `prompts/urgency.md`\n- `config/registry.yaml` — project and alias definitions\n- `config/monitoring.yaml` — ambient monitoring rules\n- `data/components.db` — versioned HTML/CSS card templates (SQLite)\n\nA spoken instruction like \"the deploy card is too cluttered\" triggers the `SelfModificationAgent`, which generates a diff, surfaces it for approval, and hot-reloads the artifact. No redeploy needed.\n\n### Project registry\n`config/registry.yaml` defines explicit project entries with cluster targets, namespaces, aliases, and workflow templates. aide-de-camp also auto-discovers git repos at startup. Refresh via `POST /api/v1/environment/refresh`.\n\n### Session persistence\nSession state lives in a SQLite WAL database (`data/session.db`). Tables: `sessions`, `surfaces`, `utterances`, `intents`, `topics`, `results`. The same session is accessible from the canvas, CLI, or audio mode simultaneously.\n\n---\n\n## Quick Start\n\n```bash\n# Clone and install\ngit clone <repo-url> aide-de-camp\ncd aide-de-camp\n\npip install -e \".[dev]\"\n# or\npip install -r requirements.txt\n\n# Start the server\nuvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000\n\n# Verify it's running\ncurl -s http://localhost:8000/health\n```\n\nOpen `http://localhost:8000/` in your browser to use the canvas.\n\nFor voice mode, an OpenAI API key with Realtime API access is required (set `OPENAI_API_KEY`).\n\n---\n\n## CLI Usage\n\n```bash\n# Route an utterance and stream results to the canvas\nadc dispatch \"has the options pipeline caught up?\"\n\n# Query a specific topic\nadc ask \"what name candidates did we have for pdftract?\"\n\n# Current session summary\nadc status\n\n# List active topic cards\nadc topics\n\n# Show exception queue\nadc exceptions\n\n# Configure the CLI\nadc config --set-server http://myhost:8000\nadc config --set-session <session-id>\n\n# Help\nadc --help\n```\n\n---\n\n## Configuration\n\n### Environment variables\n\n| Variable | Purpose | Default |\n|----------|---------|---------|\n| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI key — required for voice/Realtime API | _(none; voice disabled without it)_ |\n| `ADC_SERVER_URL` | Server URL used by the `adc` CLI | `http://localhost:8000` |\n| `ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL` | Telegram bridge URL for async task notifications | `https://telegram-proxy-telegram-bridge-ardenone-cluster-ts.ardenone.com:8444` |\n\nThe LLM backend for intent routing and synthesis is configurable via `config/registry.yaml`. By default it points to a local proxy endpoint — update this to any OpenAI-compatible API endpoint.\n\n### Prompts directory\n\nAll prompts are hot-reloaded on every invocation. Edit these files to tune behavior without restarting the server:\n\n| File | Purpose |\n|------|---------|\n| `prompts/router.md` | Intent classification and thread segmentation |\n| `prompts/synthesize.md` | Result synthesis (raw data → 2–3 sentence summary) |\n| `prompts/voice.md` | Narration style and pacing |\n| `prompts/urgency.md` | Urgency classification rules |\n\n### Project registry\n\n`config/registry.yaml` defines the projects aide-de-camp knows about. Each entry specifies:\n- `name` and `aliases` — how the router maps utterance fragments to projects\n- `cluster` and `namespace` — where to run kubectl-based fetch commands\n- `workflow_templates` — named multi-step workflows (e.g., \"deploy\", \"rollback\")\n\n---\n\n## Architecture\n\n### Key source files\n\n| File | Responsibility |\n|------|---------------|\n| `src/main.py` | FastAPI app; `/dispatch`, `/voice`, `/sse` endpoints |\n| `src/intent/router.py` | LLM classification and parallel thread dispatch |\n| `src/fetch/commands.py` | Fetch command matrix per intent type |\n| `src/fetch/orchestrator.py` | Parallel fetch execution |\n| `src/synthesize/strand.py` | LLM synthesis strand |\n| `src/session/store.py` | SQLite session store (aiosqlite) |\n| `src/sse/broadcaster.py` | SSE connection registry and event routing |\n| `src/canvas/index.html` | Canvas single-page app |\n| `src/realtime/session.py` | OpenAI Realtime API voice session |\n| `src/watcher/daemon.py` | Async work-item watcher — pushes completed results to canvas |\n| `src/agents/self_modification.py` | Self-improvement agent |\n| `src/environment/discovery.py` | Local and remote repo scanner |\n| `config/registry.yaml` | Explicit project registry |\n| `prompts/` | Hot-reload prompt files |\n\n### Runtime breakdown\n\n| Runtime | Used for |\n|---------|---------|\n| Realtime API | Voice model (persistent session, voice I/O) |\n| Configurable LLM backend | Intent router, synthesize strand |\n| Deterministic code | Fetch strand (kubectl/git execution), bead watcher |\n| Async worker fleet | UI-regen agent, self-modification agent, background analysis, task work items |\n\n### Data flow\n\n```\nPOST /dispatch\n  → intent router (LLM classify)\n  → N parallel: fetch strand → synthesize strand\n  → persist: topic + result in session.db\n  → broadcast SSEEvent(\"result_created\", surface_id)\n  → canvas calls GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics\n  → cards rendered / updated in place\n```\n\n---\n\n## Requirements\n\n- Python 3.11+\n- `fastapi`, `uvicorn[standard]`, `httpx`, `websockets`, `pydantic`, `aiosqlite`\n- Dev: `pytest`, `pytest-asyncio`, `ruff`\n\n---\n\n## Versioning\n\nVersion is in `pyproject.toml` only. No CI build — runs from source.\n\nRelease: bump version in `pyproject.toml` → commit → `git tag vX.Y.Z` → push.\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":140,"oldLines":7,"newStart":140,"newLines":7,"lines":[" |----------|---------|---------|"," | `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI key — required for voice/Realtime API | _(none; voice disabled without it)_ |"," | `ADC_SERVER_URL` | Server URL used by the `adc` CLI | `http://localhost:8000` |","-| `ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL` | Telegram bridge URL for async task notifications | `https://telegram-proxy-telegram-bridge-ardenone-cluster-ts.ardenone.com:8444` |","+| `ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL` | Telegram bridge URL for async task notifications | `https://telegram-proxy-telegram-bridge-ardenone-cluster-ts.ardenone.com:8444` ✓ |"," "," The LLM backend for intent routing and synthesis is configurable via `config/registry.yaml`.
